tasks.title: Build a personal control-room intro system
tasks.rationale: The current portfolio is polished but reads too much like a generic product site. A stronger first-screen narrative can better unify Sharang's AI engineering depth, social-impact motivation, and creative interests without changing the site's one-page structure.
acceptanceCriteria: The first viewport clearly explains who Sharang is, what he is building now, and why his work matters.
The intro uses factual personal signals that connect AI/product work with games, sports, and board-game interests.
Desktop and mobile layouts remain responsive and visually intentional.
Dark mode, light mode, and keyboard focus states remain accessible and legible.
The resulting design avoids generic SaaS styling and avoids adding unsupported claims.
notes: Implemented a new control-room hero, refreshed the profile rail, tightened narrative copy, and reframed the Games section so it reads as part of Sharang's identity.
Added stronger focus states and keyboard handling for interactive elements, including game cards and timeline links.
Updated metadata to better match the portfolio's AI-engineer-plus-creative-builder positioning.
Fixed a Mixpanel initialization crash in App.js so analytics calls do not break portfolio interactions when the token is unavailable.

verification.test: no matching tests: react-scripts reported no matching test files for the current repo glob
verification.chromeDesktop: passed: verified hero layout, games section, and game modal in headless Chrome at 1440px width
verification.chromeMobile: passed: verified hero layout, games section, and outbound game link behavior in headless Chrome at 390px width
verification.overflowCheck: passed: DOM scrollWidth matched clientWidth on both desktop and mobile viewports
